Change has been successfully rebased and submitted as c391dd18545ad9d07e76af1c9cf41dfb7c8d65b1

9 is the latest approved patch-set.
The change was submitted with unreviewed changes in the following files:

```
The name of the file: src/dawn/common/Compiler.h
Insertions: 18, Deletions: 0.

@@ -142,6 +142,10 @@
 #endif
 
 // DAWN_MUTEX_CAPABILITY
+//
+// Used when a class provides a mutex capability for thread-safety analysis.
+//
+// https://clang.llvm.org/docs/ThreadSafetyAnalysis.html#capability-string
 #if DAWN_HAS_ATTRIBUTE(capability)
 #define DAWN_MUTEX_CAPABILITY __attribute__((capability("mutex")))
 #else
@@ -149,6 +153,10 @@
 #endif
 
 // DAWN_SCOPED_LOCKABLE
+//
+// Used when a class implements RAII-style locking.
+//
+// https://clang.llvm.org/docs/ThreadSafetyAnalysis.html#scoped-capability
 #if DAWN_HAS_ATTRIBUTE(scoped_lockable)
 #define DAWN_SCOPED_LOCKABLE __attribute__((scoped_lockable))
 #else
@@ -156,6 +164,11 @@
 #endif
 
 // DAWN_EXCLUSIVE_LOCK_FUNCTION
+//
+// Used when a function acquires a lock. The lock must not be in the acquired state at the beginning
+// of the function and must be in the acquired state at the end of the function.
+//
+// https://clang.llvm.org/docs/ThreadSafetyAnalysis.html#acquire-acquire-shared-release-release-shared-release-generic
 #if DAWN_HAS_ATTRIBUTE(exclusive_lock_function)
 #define DAWN_EXCLUSIVE_LOCK_FUNCTION __attribute__((exclusive_lock_function()))
 #else
@@ -163,6 +176,11 @@
 #endif
 
 // DAWN_UNLOCK_FUNCTION
+//
+// Used when a function releases a lock. The lock must be in the acquired state at the beginning of
+// the function and must be in the released state at the end of the function.
+//
+// https://clang.llvm.org/docs/ThreadSafetyAnalysis.html#acquire-acquire-shared-release-release-shared-release-generic
 #if DAWN_HAS_ATTRIBUTE(unlock_function)
 #define DAWN_UNLOCK_FUNCTION __attribute__((unlock_function()))
 #else
```

1 is the latest approved patch-set.
The change was submitted with unreviewed changes in the following files:

```
The name of the file: docs/tint/fuzzers.md
Insertions: 5, Deletions: 5.

@@ -87,8 +87,8 @@
 `-ir` mode without any provided inputs will convert the default WGSL
 inputs from [`test/tint`](../../test/tint) on-the-fly to .tirb
 files. A manually provided input of .tirb files can be provided. There
-currently no facility to detect an explicit input directory contains
-.wgsl files that need converting toto .tirb files.
+is currently no facility to detect that an explicit input directory
+contains .wgsl files that need converting to .tirb files.
 
 See below for how to generate the corpus locally
 
@@ -157,8 +157,8 @@
 The corpus generation process for a IR corpus is the same as above,
 except using the `-ir` flag.
 
-The inputs are expected to .wgsl files like the WGSL generation, but
-the output will .tirb files. Similarly to the WGSl mode generation
+The inputs are expected to be .wgsl files like the WGSL generation, but
+the output will .tirb files. Similarly to the WGSL mode generation
 inputs with `.expected.` will be filtered out.
 
 Generating a corpus based on the default WGSL tests:
@@ -191,7 +191,7 @@
 
 Minimizing a fuzzer corpus is no longer supported as part of the
 tooling. It was rarely used, since ClusterFuzz does it automatically,
-and added significant complextity to the tooling. If you need to
+and added significant complexity to the tooling. If you need to
 minimize a corpus manually please consult the libFuzzer documentation
 for [details](https://llvm.org/docs/LibFuzzer.html#corpus)
 
```

```
@@ -365,6 +365,11 @@
     const size_t addedSize = sizeWithHash - kCachedDataSize;
 
     auto DoTest = [&](const void* loadBuffer, size_t loadSize, bool expectHashValidationSuccess) {
+        // LoadOrRun requires its cache miss handler being a free function (i.e. without any
+        // capture), so the mock function has to be static to get used in it. However, using the
+        // same mock function object for different test cases might cause their expectations mixed
+        // up, so we use a static unique_ptr to ensure that each test case constructs and destructs
+        // its own mock function object.
         static std::unique_ptr<StrictMock<MockFunction<int(Blob)>>> cacheHitFn;
         static std::unique_ptr<StrictMock<MockFunction<int(CacheRequestForTesting)>>> cacheMissFn;
         constexpr int rvCacheHit = 21;
```

text:
Change has been successfully rebased and submitted as 72cec30833da888f6a6004d7ca9ab9a38e1e8079

2 is the latest approved patch-set.
The change was submitted with unreviewed changes in the following files:

```
The name of the file: src/cmake/DawnCompilerWarningFlags.cmake
Insertions: 3, Deletions: 2.

@@ -40,8 +40,9 @@
   endif()
 endif()
 if (DAWN_WEVERYTHING)
-  # This is NOT a supported configuration, Dawn is not warning-clean in this configuration.
-  # However it's maintained "best-effort" as a way to manually discover new warnings we should fix.
+  # This is NOT a supported configuration; Dawn is not currently intended to be warning-clean in
+  # this configuration. For now it is a way to manually discover new warnings we should fix.
+  # TODO(crbug.com/436300898): Improve this config and enable more (or all) warnings.
   if (CMAKE_CXX_COMPILER_ID STREQUAL "Clang")
     target_compile_options(dawn_warnings_config
       INTERFACE
@@ -83,7 +84,6 @@
 
 set(langs C CXX)
 
-# NOTE: This should be kept in sync with src/dawn/common/BUILD.gn.
 dawn_add_flag("-Wconditional-uninitialized" ${langs})
 dawn_add_flag("-Wcstring-format-directive" ${langs})
 dawn_add_flag("-Wctad-maybe-unsupported" ${langs})
@@ -97,7 +97,6 @@
 dawn_add_flag("-Winconsistent-missing-destructor-override" ${langs})
 dawn_add_flag("-Winvalid-offsetof" ${langs})
 dawn_add_flag("-Wmissing-field-initializers" ${langs})
-dawn_add_flag("-Wmissing-include-dirs" ${langs})
 dawn_add_flag("-Wnon-c-typedef-for-linkage" ${langs})
 dawn_add_flag("-Wpessimizing-move" ${langs})
 dawn_add_flag("-Wrange-loop-analysis" ${langs})
```
